perturbation.original_text: Title to, interest in, and all other rights of ownership to Confidential Information shall remain with the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Title to, interest in, and all other rights of ownership to Confidential Information shall remain with the Disclosing Party, except that the Receiving Party shall have a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use such Confidential Information for the duration of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by granting the Receiving Party a license to use the Confidential Information, which conflicts with the original statement that all ownership rights remain with the Disclosing Party. The introduction of a license implies a partial transfer of rights, directly contradicting the original assertion of complete ownership, creating ambiguity and potential for disputes over usage rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that Confidential Information shall be used only for the purposes of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties. Additionally, subject to section 4 below, the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall maintain the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own similar categories of confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances. The Receiving Party shall not make any copies of Confidential Information except as necessary to perform hereunder. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by such Party, its employees, agents, officials, representatives or consultants acting within the scope of their engagement or employment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants the Receiving Party a 'non-exclusive, royalty-free license' to use Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the assertion in the original text that 'all other rights of ownership to Confidential Information shall remain with the Disclosing Party.' This change creates a contradiction because the contradicted text outlines restrictions on the Receiving Party's use of Confidential Information, stating it 'shall be used only for the purposes of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties' and restricts disclosure to third parties. The license granted in the changed text implies a right to use the information, which conflicts with the complete ownership retained by the Disclosing Party and creates ambiguity regarding the extent of permitted use. The contradiction aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Any confidential information exchanged between HRM and the PNS “shall only be used for the pur poses of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties” and once the agreement terminates each party must “promptly return or destroy” all confidential information received from the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Any confidential information exchanged between HRM and the PNS “shall only be used for the pur poses of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties” and once the agreement terminates each party may, at their discretion, retain archival copies of the information, but must “promptly return or destroy” all active, live copies of confidential information received from the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The modification permits retention of 'archival copies' of confidential information after termination, contradicting the original strict requirement to return or destroy all such information. This creates an in-text contradiction because it's now unclear which information must be destroyed versus can be retained, leading to potential disputes regarding compliance with confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will terminate on the date and time the Agreement between the Parties over the provision of SAP Services is terminated. In the event this Agreement is terminated, and the Disclosing Party so requests, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and certify destruction of) all Confidential Information which it received from the Disclosing Party along with all copies. The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement or the return of the Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the return or destruction of all confidential information upon termination. The changed text allows for retaining 'archival copies,' directly contradicting the requirement to return or destroy all confidential information. The contradicted text also states 'all Confidential Information which it received from the Disclosing Party along with all copies' must be returned or destroyed. The changed text explicitly permits the retention of some copies, creating a clear and direct conflict as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: 10. The Parties acknowledge and understand that all documentation and information , including Confidential Information, provided to the Province of Nova Scotia or HRM, as the case may be, pursuant to this Agreement shall be kept confidential, subject to the provisions of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act , Province of Nova Scotia, and such other laws of the Province of Nova Scotia and Canada which may apply from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. The Parties acknowledge and understand that all documentation and information , including Confidential Information, provided to the Province of Nova Scotia or HRM, as the case may be, pursuant to this Agreement shall be kept confidential, subject to the provisions of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act , Province of Nova Scotia, and such other laws of the Province of Nova Scotia and Canada which may apply from time to time. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may disclose Confidential Information if required by a valid subpoena or court order.
perturbation.explanation: By introducing the "Notwithstanding the foregoing" clause permitting disclosure under subpoena or court order, an in-text contradiction is created. This directly contradicts the earlier, more general statement that all Confidential Information shall be kept confidential, subject only to specific laws. The addition creates uncertainty as to the extent of confidentiality and when it can be breached. it is contradictory as to which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that Confidential Information shall be used only for the purposes of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties. Additionally, subject to section 4 below, the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall maintain the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own similar categories of confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances. The Receiving Party shall not make any copies of Confidential Information except as necessary to perform hereunder. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by such Party, its employees, agents, officials, representatives or consultants acting within the scope of their engagement or employment.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all Confidential Information shall be kept confidential, subject only to the provisions of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act and other laws. The changed text introduces an exception allowing disclosure if required by subpoena or court order, creating a direct contradiction. The contradicted text states that Confidential Information shall not be disclosed to any third party without prior written approval. The introduction of an exception for subpoena or court order directly conflicts with this requirement, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
